title: Climate Sensor: Tipping-Point Regime-Break Law Discovery
strategies: trivial: emit the constant train-mean of y (matches the grader's constant-mean baseline -> ~0.1)

greedy: ordinary least squares on a single unbroken basis {1, x0, x1, x2*x3, x3, x0^2}; captures non-breaking drivers but a global quadratic under-fits the far-side tipping feedback -> weak extrapolation

strong: detect the break location on x0 by SSE scan, then least-squares fit the hinge basis {1, x0, x1, x2*x3, x3, max(0,x0-C)^2}, recovering the piecewise form so it extrapolates across the break; irreducible held-out noise keeps it below saturation
invalid: emit a disallowed expression (conditional/comparison) -> infeasible (0)
